items.title: Laurus Labs delivered strong FY26 results with 22.7% revenue growth and 710 bps EBITDA margin expansion to 26.1%. The company generated robust free ca...
items.description_html:
Laurus Labs delivered strong FY26 results with 22.7% revenue growth and 710 bps EBITDA margin expansion to 26.1%. The company generated robust free cash flow of ₹553.55 crores while executing ₹1,070 crores capex, resulting in significant balance sheet deleveraging (Net Debt/EBITDA from 2.42x to 1.28x).

However, standalone Q4 performance showed a 23.9% PAT decline, indicating subsidiary contributions are masking weakness in core Indian operations. Asset turnover remains low at 0.69x vs 1.1x target.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 22.7% YoY to ₹6,812.90 crores for FY26
EBITDA margin expanded by 710 bps YoY to 26.1% for
full year, Q4 margin at 28.27%

PAT grew 151.1% YoY to ₹889.85 crores, normalized growth 135.6% YoY excluding one-time gain

Operating cash flow was robust at ₹1,623.5 cr (1.82x of PAT)

Free cash flow turned positive at ₹553.55 crores vs negative ₹39.35 crores in FY25

Net Debt/EBITDA improved significantly from 2.42x to 1.28x

Capex of ₹1,070 cr was executed while generating positive FCF and deleveraging

Risk Factors

Standalone Q4 PAT declined 23.9% YoY vs consolidated growth of 20.5%, core operations
showing weakness

Asset turnover at 0.69x remains well below management target of 1.1x

Standalone Q4 revenue declined 4.2% YoY indicating weakness in Indian operations

Inventory build-up of ₹141 cr in Q4 may indicate demand softening or overstocking

items.title: Kajaria Ceramics delivered an exceptional Q4 with PAT growth of 262.6% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 785 bps to 19.18%, driven by strong operatin...
items.description_html:
Kajaria Ceramics delivered an exceptional Q4 with PAT growth of 262.6% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 785 bps to 19.18%, driven by strong operating leverage (revenue +12.4% YoY vs expenses +2.2% YoY).

The bathware turnaround is confirmed with EBIT up 247% YoY, and the new growth engine (Others segment) grew 20.3% YoY. Strong cash generation (FCF ₹536 Cr, up 92% YoY) supports the ₹297 Cr share buyback and increased dividend of ₹14/share.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 12.4% YoY in Q4 (₹1,373.35 Cr), recovering from flat growth in Q3

PAT surged 262.6% YoY to ₹156.56 Cr in Q4

EBITDA margin expanded 785 bps YoY to 19.18% in Q4

Operating cash flow was ₹663.67 Cr (OCF/PAT ratio 1.36x)

Free cash flow doubled to ₹536.33 Cr in FY26

Bathware segment turned around with EBIT of ₹44.43 Cr (up 247% YoY)

Total debt reduced by ₹45.62 Cr to ₹130.32 Cr

Capex was ₹127.34 Cr vs guidance of ~₹200 Cr (capex moderation)

Risk Factors

Exceptional items of ₹44.02 Cr in FY26 included fraud loss (₹19.81 Cr) and labour code provision (₹19.43 Cr)

Subsidiaries contributed 9.4% to revenue but only 5.9% to PAT, indicating slight drag on profitability

Finance costs increased 12.98% YoY to ₹22.63 Cr in FY26

Q4 saw inventory drawdown of ₹94.28 Cr which increases reported COGS

items.title: RRKABEL delivered strong FY26 results with 27.6% revenue growth and 58% PAT growth, driven by Wires & Cables exceeding its 18% CAGR target with 31% gr...
items.description_html:
RRKABEL delivered strong FY26 results with 27.6% revenue growth and 58% PAT growth, driven by Wires & Cables exceeding its 18% CAGR target with 31% growth. EBITDA margin improved 152 bps to 7.92%, meeting annual guidance.

However, working capital deteriorated significantly (inventories +75%, CFO -40%) and FMEG segment remains loss-making, missing breakeven target. Balance sheet remains strong with 0.09x debt-to-equity. The core W&C business shows strong execution while FMEG turnaround remains a work in progress.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 33.65% YoY in Q4 and 27.62% for FY26, exceeding W&C growth target of 18% CAGR

PAT grew 30.06% YoY in Q4 and 57.96% for FY26, with normalized PAT up 62.5% YoY

EBITDA margin improved 152 bps YoY for FY26 to 7.92%, meeting margin guidance target

W&C EBIT margin improved 143 bps to 8.85% for FY26, on track with 100 bps annual improvement target

Debt-to-equity ratio very strong at 0.09x, company maintains strong balance sheet

ROE achieved 20.67%, meeting management's >20% target

Risk Factors

FMEG segment posted EBIT loss of ₹9.3 Cr in Q4, missing breakeven target for Q4 FY26

FMEG revenue grew only 3.1% vs target of 25% CAGR, significantly underperforming

Working capital deteriorated significantly: inventories up 75.2% YoY, receivables up 21.2%

Cash conversion weak: CFO/PAT ratio at 0.60x for FY26, CFO declined 40.3% YoY

Finance costs increased 61.3% YoY in Q4, indicating higher debt/working capital needs

Q4 W&C EBIT margin declined 27 bps YoY to 9.65% from 9.92%

items.title: PSP Projects met its FY26 revenue guidance (₹3,148.66 Crore within ₹3,100-3,200 Crore range) but significantly missed EBITDA margin guidance (6.00% ...
items.description_html:
PSP Projects met its FY26 revenue guidance (₹3,148.66 Crore within ₹3,100-3,200 Crore range) but significantly missed EBITDA margin guidance (6.00% vs 8-9% target). While Q4 showed strong execution with 65.7% YoY revenue growth and 226% PAT growth, full-year profitability declined 1.6% due to cost pressure.

Operating cash flow was exceptionally strong (5.81x PAT), but working capital deterioration (75% receivables growth) and margin compression remain concerns. Execution was in-line on revenue but poor on profitability.

Positive Factors

Revenue of ₹3,148.66 Crore met management's FY26 guidance of ₹3,100-3,200 Crore

Q4 revenue grew 65.74% YoY to ₹1,115.24 Crore with 37.21% QoQ acceleration

Q4 PAT grew 226.37% YoY to ₹21.09 Crore

Operating cash flow was strong at ₹322.82 Crore with 5.81x PAT conversion

Free cash flow positive at ₹129.85 Crore despite elevated capex

Capex of ₹192.96 Crore was in line with ~₹200 Crore guidance

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin of 6.00% missed 8-9% guidance by 200-300 bps

Trade receivables surged 75.2% YoY to ₹928.22 Crore, outpacing revenue growth

Other expenses surged 94.47% YoY to ₹81.58 Crore, driving margin compression

FY26 PAT declined 1.6% YoY to ₹55.52 Crore despite 25.3% revenue growth

Working capital stress evident from receivables growth vs revenue

items.title: GHCLTEXTIL delivered in-line performance with FY26 revenue growth of 13.56% (meeting 14-15% guidance at low end) and EBITDA margin of 11.26% normalize...
items.description_html:
GHCLTEXTIL delivered in-line performance with FY26 revenue growth of 13.56% (meeting 14-15% guidance at low end) and EBITDA margin of 11.26% normalized (meeting 11% target). Q4 showed strong momentum with 28% YoY revenue growth and near-doubling of PAT.

However, significant cash flow deterioration (CFO/PAT at 0.06x) and working capital stress (inventories +42.7%, receivables +45.4%) funded by doubling debt raises concerns about earnings quality. The turnaround is confirmed with PBT up 47% YoY, but execution quality is good rather than superior given the cash conversion weakness.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue grew 28.24% YoY to ₹363.69 Cr, showing strong recovery from H1 softness

Q4 PAT nearly doubled to ₹27.66 Cr, up 94.79% YoY

FY26 revenue of ₹1,318.60 Cr met guidance at the low end of 14-15% target

EBITDA margin expanded 116 bps YoY to 10.61% (normalized 11.26% met 11% target)

Operating leverage visible with employee costs and power costs growing slower than revenue

New capacities commissioned with ₹153.12 Cr moved from CWIP to PPE

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow collapsed to ₹4.55 Cr from ₹162.16 Cr in FY25, CFO/PAT ratio only 0.06x

Working capital stress: inventories up 42.7% and receivables up 45.4% vs revenue growth of 13.6%

Borrowings more than doubled to ₹134.22 Cr to fund working capital gap

One-time asset gain of ₹8.59 Cr inflated PAT; normalized PAT grew only 14.1% vs reported 25.7%

FY27 capex budget of ₹127.77 Cr signals continued investment but may further pressure cash

items.title: GOCOLORS reported a significant miss on its FY26 guidance with revenue declining 1.2% YoY (vs positive SSSG target) and PAT falling 36.7%. The company...
items.description_html:
GOCOLORS reported a significant miss on its FY26 guidance with revenue declining 1.2% YoY (vs positive SSSG target) and PAT falling 36.7%. The company achieved its gross margin guidance but missed on all growth and profitability metrics.

Operating deleverage from 4.6% expense growth against negative revenue, combined with 13.1% inventory buildup, indicates structural challenges. Strong cash generation and share buyback provide some offset, but execution quality is poor with guidance misses across multiple dimensions.

Positive Factors

Gross margin maintained at 63.2% within guided 62-63% range

Free cash flow strong at 12,554.75 Lakhs

Operating cash flow to PAT ratio healthy at 2.78x

Completed share buyback of 14,13,000 shares at ₹460

Zero financial debt on balance sheet

ASP grew 4.4% YoY in 9M FY26 exceeding 2-3% target

Risk Factors

Revenue declined 4.25% YoY in Q4 and 1.2% for full FY26

PAT declined 60% YoY in Q4 and 36.7% for FY26

EBITDA margin contracted 330 bps to 28.3%

Employee costs grew 11.1% YoY outpacing revenue

Inventories grew 13.1% YoY indicating working capital pressure

Store additions of 49 in 9M likely missed 80-90 target

Same-store sales growth negative at -4.2% YoY in Q4

items.title: Sona Comstar reported strong revenue growth of 45.4% YoY in Q4 and 25.5% for FY26, driven by the acquisition of Escorts Kubota Railway Business. Howev...
items.description_html:
Sona Comstar reported strong revenue growth of 45.4% YoY in Q4 and 25.5% for FY26, driven by the acquisition of Escorts Kubota Railway Business.

However, margin compression of 292 bps YoY in Q4 and 357 bps for the full year, along with significant working capital deterioration and expense growth outpacing revenue, indicate execution challenges. No prior management guidance was provided in the source for comparison, so no guidance beats or misses can be determined.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 45.4% YoY in Q4 and 25.5% for full year FY26

PAT grew 16.9% YoY in Q4 to ₹1,919.15 Million

Normalized PAT grew 10.2% YoY adjusting for exceptional items

Operating cash flow to PAT ratio healthy at 1.03x

Free cash flow positive at ₹1,672.24 Million

Foreign exchange gain increased 302% YoY in Q4

Completed acquisition of Escorts Kubota Railway
Business

Mexican subsidiary expansion approved with USD 6 Million investment

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin contracted 292 bps YoY in Q4 and 357 bps for full year

Total expenses grew 48.4% YoY, outpacing revenue growth of 45.4%

Working capital deteriorated significantly - inventories up 76.5%, receivables up 63.2%

Cash and cash equivalents declined 85.6% YoY due to acquisitions

Current borrowings increased from ₹54.92 Million to ₹2,342.54 Million

Other income declined 64.4% YoY in Q4

Subsidiaries represented -0.97% drag on consolidated PAT

items.title: PCBL's Q4 showed sequential recovery but FY26 was a significant miss on all key metrics - revenue declined 2.6% vs high-single-digit growth guidance, ...
items.description_html:
PCBL's Q4 showed sequential recovery but FY26 was a significant miss on all key metrics - revenue declined 2.6% vs high-single-digit growth guidance, Carbon Black volume missed target by ~5%, and Aquapharm segment collapsed with EBIT down 73%.

The only positives were strong working capital management generating ₹1,576 Cr operating cash flow and ₹555 Cr debt reduction. Sequential improvement in Carbon Black QoQ (32.6% EBIT growth) suggests the worst may be over, but execution on guidance was poor.

Positive Factors

Sequential recovery in Q4 - revenue up 11.9% QoQ to ₹2,066 Cr

Carbon Black segment EBIT grew 32.6% QoQ indicating potential demand stabilization

Power segment delivered strong performance with revenue up 9.9% YoY and EBIT up 8% YoY

Operating cash flow more than doubled to ₹1,576 Cr driven by ₹529 Cr working capital release

Debt reduced by ₹555 Cr enabling balance sheet strengthening

Free cash flow of ₹850 Cr generated after capex of ₹727 Cr

Capacity expansion completed - 90,000 MTPA added in Tamil Nadu

Risk Factors

Revenue declined 2.6% YoY missing 'high single digit' growth guidance

PAT declined 59.9% YoY in Q4 and 54.4% for full year

Carbon Black EBIT margins contracted 425 bps for full year to 11.07%

Chemical segment (Aquapharm) EBIT fell 73% YoY, Q4 loss of ₹8.08 Cr vs guidance of ₹70-80 Cr EBITDA run-rate

Carbon Black volume estimated at 619,000 MT vs 650,000 MT target

Working capital position deteriorated - current ratio at 0.83x below 1

Subsidiaries dragged consolidated PBT lower by ₹61 Cr vs standalone

items.title: IDBI Bank reported mixed Q4 results with full year PAT growth of 26.59% supported by one-off NSDL gain and lower tax rate, but Q4 showed weakness with...
items.description_html:
IDBI Bank reported mixed Q4 results with full year PAT growth of 26.59% supported by one-off NSDL gain and lower tax rate, but Q4 showed weakness with PAT declining 5.27% YoY. Key positives include strong capital adequacy (CAR 26.65%), improving asset quality (GNPA 2.32%), and Treasury turnaround.

Concerns center on cost inflation outpacing revenue growth, deterioration in core banking segments (Corporate and Retail PBT down significantly), and margin compression. No management guidance was provided for comparison, limiting alpha assessment.

Positive Factors

Full year PAT grew 26.59% YoY to ₹9,513.36 Cr

NII grew 17.07% YoY in Q4 to ₹3,851.42 Cr

GNPA ratio improved to 2.32% from 2.98% YoY

Net NPA ratio stable at very low 0.15%

CAR at 26.65% and CET1 at 25.56% - exceptionally strong capital

Treasury segment swung to profit of ₹589.61 Cr in Q4 from loss of ₹766.36 Cr

Provision Coverage Ratio at 99.39% - very robust buffer

Net Advances grew 16% YoY, Deposits grew 12% YoY

Risk Factors

Q4 PAT declined 5.27% YoY to ₹1,943.17 Cr

Q4 PPOP declined 4.74% YoY to ₹3,043.38 Cr

Other Income declined 21.67% YoY in Q4

Operating expenses grew 12.42% YoY, outpacing revenue growth

Cost-to-income ratio worsened to 46.50% from 43.33%

Corporate banking PBT declined 62.88% YoY in Q4

Retail banking PBT declined 38.52% YoY in Q4
Full year NII declined 7.62% YoY

items.title: EDELWEISS FY26 showed mixed results with strong PAT growth of 27% meeting guidance, but Q4 was weak with PBT loss of ₹29 Cr due to fair value losses....
items.description_html:
EDELWEISS FY26 showed mixed results with strong PAT growth of 27% meeting guidance, but Q4 was weak with PBT loss of ₹29 Cr due to fair value losses. Key concerns include insurance business widening losses (off track for FY27 breakeven), MSME disbursements missing target by 58%, and Capital business segment volatility.

Operational KPIs in Housing Finance, General Insurance, and Asset Reconstruction showed strong growth, indicating underlying business momentum despite one-time headwinds.

Positive Factors

FY26 PAT grew 27% YoY to ₹680.46 Cr, meeting guidance of 20-30% growth

Net Interest Income grew 21.6% YoY in Q4 indicating improved lending spreads

Total Income grew 14.1% YoY to ₹10,865.14 Cr for FY26

Housing Finance disbursals grew 27% YoY, MSME disbursals tripled YoY

Standalone entity turned profitable with ₹162.32 Cr PAT in Q4 vs loss of ₹1.65 Cr in Q4 FY25

Mutual Fund Equity AUM grew 25% YoY to ₹78,000 Cr

General Insurance GWP grew 28% YoY to ₹1,294 Cr

ARC Recoveries grew 50% YoY to ₹8,590 Cr

Risk Factors

Q4 PBT turned negative at ₹(29.02) Cr due to fair value loss of ₹235.05 Cr in Capital business

Insurance business loss widened to ₹216.31 Cr in FY26 from ₹175.28 Cr in FY25, off track for FY27 breakeven

MSME disbursement target of ₹2,000-2,500 Cr missed at ₹1,051 Cr (58% miss)

Alternatives business missed 25% profitability target, grew only 19.1% YoY

Capital business posted PBT loss of ₹263.93 Cr in Q4

Mutual Fund Equity AUM of ₹78,000 Cr behind target of ₹100,000 Cr

Cash flow from operations declined to ₹897.26 Cr from ₹3,383.76 Cr in FY25

items.title: Adani Enterprises reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong Q4 revenue growth of 20.3% driven by new businesses like Copper and Airport, but core pro...
items.description_html:
Adani Enterprises reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong Q4 revenue growth of 20.3% driven by new businesses like Copper and Airport, but core profitability deteriorated sharply with Q4 net loss of ₹220.71 crore. Full-year performance was heavily inflated by ₹9,215 crore exceptional gains from AWL stake sale, while normalized PAT declined 36% YoY.

The Road segment significantly missed guidance (EBIT down 46% vs doubling target), and working capital deterioration led to negative free cash flow of ₹31,012 crore. The rights issue improved balance sheet leverage but execution quality remains poor with multiple guidance misses.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 20.3% YoY to ₹32,439.31 crore in Q4 FY26

Copper segment scaled to ₹9,145.43 crore revenue (579.5% YoY), becoming the largest segment

Airport segment revenue up 27.4% YoY with EBIT nearly tripling in Q4

Full-year FY26 EBITDA grew 27.5% YoY with 451 bps margin expansion to 23.1%

Successfully raised ₹24,854 crore through rights issue, improving D/E ratio from 1.35x to 0.97x

Achieved 93% of capex target (₹33,369 crore vs ₹36,000 crore guidance)

Risk Factors

Q4 FY26 recorded net loss of ₹220.71 crore vs profit of ₹3,844.91 crore in Q4 FY25

Normalized PAT declined 36% YoY to ₹2,735 crore indicating core operating weakness

Road segment EBIT collapsed 46% YoY to ₹679 crore vs guidance to double, Q4 EBIT near zero

Commercial Mining segment posted large EBIT loss of ₹1,127.91 crore in Q4

Working capital deterioration: inventories up ₹8,340 crore, receivables up ₹5,300 crore

Operating cash flow declined 47.8% YoY to ₹2,357 crore with FCF negative ₹31,012 crore

Auditor qualification on financial results related to MIAL investigation

items.title: Adani Ports delivered solid FY26 results with revenue and EBITDA meeting management guidance, though Q4 saw significant margin compression due to surg...
items.description_html:
Adani Ports delivered solid FY26 results with revenue and EBITDA meeting management guidance, though Q4 saw significant margin compression due to surging operating expenses and finance costs. Balance sheet strengthened with Debt Equity ratio improving to 0.57 and strong cash generation.

The emerging logistics business (55.7% growth) provides a new growth driver, but margin pressure and market share loss remain concerns. Management's FY29 target of doubling revenue/EBITDA from FY25 base remains on track.

Positive Factors

Revenue of ₹38,735.77 Cr beat management guidance of ₹38,000 Cr by 1.9%

EBITDA of ₹22,851.41 Cr met management guidance of ₹22,800 Cr

PAT grew 15.6% YoY to ₹12,782 Cr with EPS of ₹58.23

Operating cash flow strong at ₹20,356 Cr with 1.59x CFO to PAT conversion

Debt Equity Ratio improved to 0.57 from 0.73, balance sheet strengthened

Current Ratio improved to 1.39x from 0.90x, liquidity enhanced

Free cash flow positive at ₹5,036 Cr despite capex of ₹15,320 Cr

Logistics 'Others' segment grew 55.7% YoY, new growth driver emerging

Risk Factors

Operating expenses surged 44.3% YoY in Q4,
outpacing revenue growth of 26.5%

Finance costs rose 103.8% YoY in Q4, reflecting higher debt for acquisitions

Q4 EBITDA margin compressed to 46.8% from 59.0% in Q3 (1220 bps QoQ decline)

Port & SEZ segment EBIT margin contracted 555 bps YoY to 47.5% in Q4

Market share declined 60 bps YoY to 26.4% in Q3
Interest Service Coverage Ratio declined to 5.89x from 6.87x

items.title: Usha Martin delivered a strong FY26 with EBITDA margin of 19.6% beating the 18% guidance by 160 bps, and operating EBITDA of ₹723.6 Crore exceeding t...
items.description_html:
Usha Martin delivered a strong FY26 with EBITDA margin of 19.6% beating the 18% guidance by 160 bps, and operating EBITDA of ₹723.6 Crore exceeding the ₹680-700 Crore range.

The balance sheet transformation was dramatic - moving from net debt of ₹63 Crore to net cash of ₹332 Crore. However, revenue growth of 6.2% lagged the 12-15% guidance target.

The core Wire & Wire Ropes segment showed exceptional margin expansion of 557 bps YoY in Q4, demonstrating successful execution on value-added product mix strategy.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 9.3% YoY to ₹979.3 Crore, full year revenue ₹3,691.1 Crore up 6.2%

FY26 EBITDA margin expanded 190 bps to 19.6% vs guidance of 18%

Q4 PAT grew 46.4% YoY to ₹147.8 Crore, full year PAT ₹466 Crore up 14.4%

Balance sheet transformed: net debt of ₹63 Crore to net cash of ₹332 Crore

Operating cash flow strong at ₹655 Crore, up 55.4% YoY, CFO/PAT ratio 1.4x

Wire & Wire Ropes segment EBIT margin expanded 557 bps YoY in Q4 to 20.6%

Finance costs declined 46.2% YoY in Q4 due to deleveraging

Risk Factors

Revenue growth of 6.2% missed management guidance of 12-15%

Others segment (Telecommunication Cables) revenue declined 22% YoY, EBIT turned negative

Ongoing ED and CBI investigations related to mining lease terms remain a contingent liability

One-time exceptional charge of ₹16.9 Crore for New Labour Codes impact

items.title: NATIONALUM delivered a strong FY26 with revenue of ₹17,843 crore (6.3% YoY) and PAT of ₹5,797 crore (10% YoY), significantly beating its own guidanc...
items.description_html:
NATIONALUM delivered a strong FY26 with revenue of ₹17,843 crore (6.3% YoY) and PAT of ₹5,797 crore (10% YoY), significantly beating its own guidance on revenue (>₹15,000 crore) and EBITDA margin (48.16% vs 36-37% guidance).

The company demonstrated superior execution with cost savings from captive coal production driving 131 bps margin expansion, while the Aluminium segment offset weakness in the Chemicals segment. Operating cash flow of ₹6,438 crore with 1.11x conversion and robust FCF of ₹4,400 crore underscore high earnings quality despite Q4 YoY declines.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 6.3% YoY to ₹17,843 crore, beating guidance of >₹15,000 crore

FY26 EBITDA margin expanded 131 bps to 48.16%, significantly exceeding guidance of 36-37%

Aluminium segment delivered strong performance with 16.5% revenue growth and 37.5% EBIT growth YoY

Power & fuel costs declined 28.3% YoY in Q4 due to captive coal production

Operating cash flow grew 10.9% YoY to ₹6,438 crore with 1.11x cash conversion ratio

Alumina production increased by 2.25 lakh tonnes YoY to 23 lakh tonnes, meeting guidance

Free cash flow was robust at ₹4,400 crore after funding ₹2,038 crore capex

Cash reserves increased 54.9% YoY to ₹8,408 crore

Risk Factors

Chemicals segment EBIT declined 70.4% YoY in Q4 due to severe alumina price decline

Chemicals segment margins compressed 2745 bps YoY to 25.1% in Q4

Revenue declined 4.8% YoY in Q4 due to high base quarter and Chemicals segment weakness

Other expenses increased 30.8% YoY in Q4, significantly outpacing revenue growth

Full year finance costs rose 69.2% to ₹99.80 crore

items.title: CHOLAFIN delivered strong earnings growth with PAT up 30.6% YoY, driven by Net Income expansion outpacing finance cost growth. The company met its AUM...
items.description_html:
CHOLAFIN delivered strong earnings growth with PAT up 30.6% YoY, driven by Net Income expansion outpacing finance cost growth. The company met its AUM and disbursement guidance while new growth segments (SME +41%, SBPL +46%) are gaining traction.

Asset quality showed a sequential inflection with Stage 3 improving to 3.05% from 3.36% in Q3, though YoY comparison remains weak. Credit cost and PBT-ROTA missed guidance, creating some headroom concerns, but strong capital position and improving provision coverage provide buffers.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 30.6% YoY to ₹1,645.20 crores, beating total income growth of 20%

Net Income grew 27.4% YoY to ₹4,789.15 crores,
outpacing finance cost growth of 11.1%

PPOP grew 27.6% YoY in Q4 and 27.2% for FY26

AUM grew 21.4% YoY to ₹2,42,630 crores, within management's 20-22% guidance

Disbursement growth of 10.7% YoY exceeded management's 10% target

Stage 3 assets improved sequentially to 3.05% from 3.36% in Q3

Provision Coverage Ratio improved to 47.29% from 43.03% in Q3

CAR at 19.21% remains well above regulatory requirement of 15%

New segments (SME +41% YoY, SBPL +46% YoY)
growing faster than core Vehicle Finance (+18%)

Risk Factors

Credit cost came in at 1.77% vs management's guidance of 1.6% - missed target

PBT-ROTA of 3.12% missed management's conservative target of 3.4%

Gross Stage 3 deteriorated YoY to 3.05% from 2.81%

Management overlay provision of ₹200 crores impacted reported profitability

YoY asset quality remains weaker than prior year despite sequential improvement

items.title: Bajaj Finserv delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong full-year performance masked by quarterly volatility. The retail financing segment (Bajaj Fi...
items.description_html:
Bajaj Finserv delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong full-year performance masked by quarterly volatility. The retail financing segment (Bajaj Finance) drove 28.5% YoY PBT growth and 22% AUM expansion, while the insurance segment reported a Q4 loss due to MTM losses on investments.

Life insurance new business metrics remain strong (VNB +29% YoY), indicating future profitability potential. The company missed its FY26 AUM growth and credit cost guidance targets, though asset quality remained controlled. Normalized FY26 profit growth of 18.4% YoY demonstrates strong underlying performance ex-one-offs.

Positive Factors

Full-year FY26 total income grew 13.21% YoY to ₹1,50,530.38 Cr

Normalized PAT grew 18.4% YoY to ₹10,507.97 Cr after adjusting for one-time charges

Retail financing PBT grew 28.5% YoY to ₹7,218.61 Cr in Q4, driving group profitability

Bajaj Finance AUM grew 22% YoY to ₹5,09,975 Cr

Life insurance VNB grew 29% YoY and New Business Premium grew 26% YoY in Q4

GNPA maintained at 1.01%, within guidance of <1.2%

Completed acquisition of 100% stake in insurance subsidiaries from Allianz

Risk Factors

Insurance segment reported Q4 PBT loss of ₹10.24 Cr vs profit of ₹648.05 Cr YoY due to MTM losses

Life insurance PBT swung to loss of ₹137.70 Cr from profit of ₹141.76 Cr YoY

General insurance PBT declined 74.8% YoY to ₹127.46 Cr with combined ratio at 113.6%

Bajaj Finance missed AUM growth guidance (22% vs 24-25% target)

Net loss on fair value change of ₹3,250.82 Cr in Q4 vs gain of ₹330.68 Cr in Q4 FY25

Effective tax rate increased to 24.56% from 20.76% YoY

items.title: 5PAISA reported strong Q4 FY26 with 19.9% YoY revenue growth and 7.8% PAT growth, but full-year performance declined significantly with 11.1% revenue ...
items.description_html:
5PAISA reported strong Q4 FY26 with 19.9% YoY revenue growth and 7.8% PAT growth, but full-year performance declined significantly with 11.1% revenue and 35.2% PAT decline. The company has no explicit guidance policy, so no guidance beats can be claimed.

Major concerns include severely negative operating cash flow (-2.35x CFO/PAT), 74.5% YoY surge in finance costs, and 21% decline in fees income. The ₹468.82 crore rights issue completed post-quarter end strengthens the capital base but cash conversion remains a critical issue.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 19.9% YoY to ₹8,542.28 lakhs showing strong recovery momentum

Q4 PAT grew 7.8% YoY to ₹1,085.52 lakhs

Sequential revenue growth of 7.8% QoQ indicates continued recovery

Net worth grew 7.55% YoY to ₹64,922.42 lakhs

Rights issue of ₹468.82 crore completed, significantly enhancing capital base

Balance sheet remains healthy with low leverage (0.58x debt-equity)

Customer base expanded from 4.83 lakh to 50.8 lakh over the year

ADTO grew 24% QoQ in Q3 FY26 showing strong trading activity recovery

Risk Factors

Full year FY26 revenue declined 11.1% YoY to ₹31,955.66 lakhs

Full year PAT declined 35.2% YoY to ₹4,418.55 lakhs

Severely negative operating cash flow of (₹10,367.68) lakhs in FY26 with CFO/PAT ratio of -2.35x

Finance costs rose 74.5% YoY in Q4 and 36.7% for full year

EBITDA margin contracted 294 bps YoY to 19.47% in Q4

Fees and commission income declined 21% YoY for full year

Customer acquisition slowed 18% QoQ in Q3 to 0.78 lakh

IT search show-cause notice received creating contingent liability

items.title: HFCL delivered a strong turnaround in Q4 FY26 with revenue growing 127.8% YoY and PAT swinging to ₹184.45 Cr from loss. The telecom segment showed ex...
items.description_html:
HFCL delivered a strong turnaround in Q4 FY26 with revenue growing 127.8% YoY and PAT swinging to ₹184.45 Cr from loss. The telecom segment showed exceptional performance with EBIT margin of 31.8%.

However, the company missed its EBITDA margin guidance (16.70% vs 18-20% target) and faces working capital pressure with negative CFO of ₹378 Cr. The record order book of ₹21,206 Cr provides strong revenue visibility for FY27, but execution of the loss-making Turnkey Contracts segment remains a key challenge.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 127.8% YoY to ₹1,824.12 Cr in Q4, with FY26 revenue up 21.77% to ₹4,949.27 Cr

Telecom segment EBIT surged to ₹383 Cr from ₹4.52 Cr in Q4 FY25, delivering 31.8% EBIT margin

EBITDA margin expanded 2,126 bps YoY to 18.47% in Q4, showing strong operating leverage

Order book reached record ₹21,206 Cr, nearly doubling from ₹11,125 Cr in Q3, driven by $1.1B supply deal

Export revenue increased to 41% of total revenue in FY26 from 12% in FY25, improving geographic mix

PAT swung from loss of ₹83.30 Cr in Q4 FY25 to profit of ₹184.45 Cr

Risk Factors

Turnkey Contracts segment reported EBIT loss of ₹84.10 Cr in Q4, widening from ₹56.43 Cr loss in Q4
FY25

Defence segment posted operating loss of ₹5.90 Cr in Q4 with revenue only at ₹12.42 Cr

Operating cash flow negative at ₹378.13 Cr for FY26 despite strong PAT, indicating working capital stress

EBITDA margin of 16.70% missed management's guided range of 18-20%

Net margin of 6.30% fell short of management's target of ~10%

Finance costs increased 30.8% YoY to ₹242 Cr, reflecting higher debt for growth funding

items.title: ACUTAAS delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue growth of 33.0% (vs ~30% guidance) and EBITDA margin of 35.87% (vs 28-30% guidance range)...
items.description_html:
ACUTAAS delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue growth of 33.0% (vs ~30% guidance) and EBITDA margin of 35.87% (vs 28-30% guidance range), representing 302 bps and 587 bps upside respectively.

PAT more than doubled to ₹356.4 Cr with strong operating leverage (revenue +41% YoY vs expenses +12%). The company is executing a heavy capex cycle (CWIP +155%) for new growth drivers while maintaining strong cash conversion (0.82x CFO/PAT).

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 33.0% YoY to ₹1,339.4 Cr, exceeding management's ~30% guidance by 302 bps

EBITDA margin expanded 1282 bps to 35.87%, beating 28-30% guidance range by 587 bps

PAT more than doubled to ₹356.4 Cr, up 122.2% YoY

Operating cash flow doubled to ₹292.2 Cr with 0.82x CFO/PAT conversion

CWIP up 155% YoY to ₹332.3 Cr, indicating capacity expansion on track

Equity grew 29.6% YoY to ₹1,710.8 Cr, funded by strong internal accruals

Risk Factors

Subsidiaries created 2.8% PAT drag (₹9.9 Cr) in FY26, Baba Fine Chemicals business remains soft

New businesses (semiconductor JV) not yet profitable, expected H2 FY27

Product concentration risk: single segment operations, new ventures currently a drag not yet de-risking

items.description_html:
Looks like another big deal is brewing in the consumer brands space.

Wishcare, a seven-year-old company known for its hair and skincare products, is looking for a buyer at a Rs 3,000-3,500 crore valuation.

This represents over a 20x jump from its last valuation of Rs 141 crore in late 2023, when Unilever Ventures led a Rs 20 crore round - it hasn’t raised any other funding since.

In FY24, it's revenues stood at Rs 70 crore.

In FY25, revenues grew 187% to Rs 202 crore, with a net profit of Rs 51 crore - a massive margin.

The company has reached Rs 350 crore in revenue and Rs 100 crore in EBITDA in FY26, according to the report.

items.title: RT Sawyer Merritt: Here's a rough summary of Elon Musk’s 1 hour and 40 minute long testimony today during the OpenAI trial. He will resume his testim...
items.description_html:
RT Sawyer Merritt
Here's a rough summary of Elon Musk’s 1 hour and 40 minute long testimony today during the OpenAI trial. He will resume his testimony tomorrow.

• Argues the case has huge implications: "It is not ok to steal a charity. If the defendants are found not guilty, this case will become caselaw. It’ll give license to looting every charity in America. The consequences of this case go far beyond me or everyone here. The entire foundation of charitable giving in America will be destroyed."

• Says OpenAI was founded as a nonprofit, open-source counterweight to Google, focused on AI safety.

• Claims the shift to a for-profit structure violated that mission.

• Says his AI concerns date back to conversations with Larry Page, who he felt wasn’t taking AI risk seriously.

• Elon says he tried to warn Obama about AI, but that Obama felt AI was not good enough (back then) to seem scary smart. "Here we are in 2026, AI is very smart."

• Believes AI could surpass human intelligence as soon as next year and poses existential risk in the hands of the wrong people: “If you have someone who’s not very trustworthy in charge of AI, that’s very dangerous for the whole world.”

• Elon framed his companies (SpaceX, Neuralink, xAI) as part of a broader mission to protect humanity’s future.

• Emphasized OpenAI’s original goal: AI for the good of humanity, not profit-driven control.

• Elon's main argument is that OpenAI abandoned its founding principles, and that precedent could reshape both AI governance and charitable trust.

• Larry Page refused to speak to Elon Musk again after Elon recruited Ilya Sutskever to join OpenAI. Elon viewed Ilya as the “number one” most valuable member at Google.

• Elon thought in the early days, OpenAI's corporate structure would be a nonprofit funded initially with donations, but there could potentially be a parallel for-profit that is owned by the nonprofit and funds the nonprofit: “We (Sam Altman and Elon) were in agreement that OpenAI would be a 501c3 charity.

• Elon was not opposed to there being a small for-profit that provided funding to the nonprofit, as long as "the tail didn’t wag the dog."

• Elon: “there are very few people who understand venture capital in Silicon Valley like I do."

items.title: Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit: “The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI ass...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit:

“The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI assistants are a real but early segment, and currently nowhere close to disrupting our business.”

He adds that, just as Google was not able to disrupt consumer behaviour in travel booking (Expedia, Booking) and shopping (Amazon), general AI chat interfaces will not be able to disrupt “complex, high-frequency” transactions or the “brand associations” tied to specific apps.

“Our moat remains the physical world. AI makes that moat wider by allowing more of India to participate in it.”

More in the letter on how Eternal is using and thinking about AI.

items.title: RT Bill Bishop: It was a sarcastic tweet. PRC has big leverage over meta and Zuckerberg. https://sinocism.com/p/managing-new-employment-groups-ndrc an...
items.description_html:
RT Bill Bishop
It was a sarcastic tweet. PRC has big leverage over meta and Zuckerberg. https://sinocism.com/p/managing-new-employment-groups-ndrc and this cave should raise even more alarm bells about the meta smart glasses/surveillance devices being made in China


Bill Bishop: Why is Meta just caving and not hiring lobbying firms and law firms to influence and sue the PRC government like they would if they got this kind of order from the US government?

items.title: Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV). Zomato has seen net order ...
items.description_html:
Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV).

Zomato has seen net order value (NOV) growth improve from 13.1% YoY in the June 2025 quarter to 18.8% in the March 2026 quarter.

The key drivers include lowering the free delivery threshold to Rs 99 from Rs 199 and pushing meals priced under Rs 250.

This has increased the number of monthly transacting customers (MTCs) by over 20%, from 20.9 million to 25.4 million over the last nine months.

“Order volume growth is being driven by MTC growth, both from new-to-platform customers and higher frequency among existing budget-conscious customers.”

Deepinder Goyal says that this has decreased AOV, but revenue per order continues to improve.
